themes.label: Covenant
themes.summary: This chapter points to covenant, promise, oath, inheritance, or the enduring faithfulness of God. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 2 verses. Commentary leans on "The 'covenant of peace' echoes other eternal covenants God made (with Noah, Abraham, David)....". Cross references echo "Covenant".

evidenceTrail.label: Verse 12 commentary
evidenceTrail.detail: The 'covenant of peace' echoes other eternal covenants God made (with Noah, Abraham, David)....

evidenceTrail.label: Verse 12 word study
evidenceTrail.detail: בְּרִיתִ֖י: unto him my covenant - a compact (because made by passing between pieces of flesh)

evidenceTrail.label: Verse 13 commentary
evidenceTrail.detail: What faithfulness in crisis might secure blessing not just for you but for your descendants?

themes.label: Peace/Rest
themes.summary: This chapter offers peace, rest, stillness, comfort, or settled safety in God. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 1 verse. Commentary leans on "God announced He was giving Phinehas 'my covenant of peace'—seemingly paradoxical since Phine...". Word-study detail reinforces "שָׁלֽוֹם׃: of peace - safe, i.e., (figuratively) well, happy, friendly; also (abstractly) wel...".

themes.label: Creation
themes.summary: This chapter underscores creation, the heavens and earth, or God’s creative ordering work. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 1 verse. Commentary leans on "The covenant gave Phinehas and his descendants 'an everlasting priesthood; because he was zea...". Word-study detail reinforces "וַיְכַפֵּ֖ר: and made an atonement - to cover (specifically with bitumen)".
